title: ‘Adulting looks cheaper, but…’: Pune woman shares monthly expense of family of 3: From ₹31,000 rent to ₹16,000 for nanny
summary: A Pune woman shared monthly expenses of family of 3 living in Baner with two earning members. From ₹31,000 rent to ₹16,000 for nanny, let's have a look at the full list of expenditures.

title: ITR Filing AY 2026-27: Don't miss any bank account while filing your tax return. Here's why
summary: The ITR forms for AY 2026-27 require taxpayers to disclose all Indian bank accounts held during FY 2025-26, except officially dormant accounts. Accurate bank details are also essential for timely income tax refunds.

title: Looking to invest in debt funds for a 1-3 year horizon? Expert shares 4 categories you can consider
summary: For a 1-3 year investment horizon, short-duration, dynamic bond, corporate bond, and banking &amp; PSU funds are among the best debt fund categories you can consider. Unlike others, credit risk funds invest in lower-rated bonds, making them riskier.

title: Bank seized your property after loan default? RBI's new rules explained
summary: The Reserve Bank of India has issued a new framework governing how banks must value, manage and sell properties acquired while recovering defaulted loans. The rules, effective October 1, 2026, also bar banks from selling such properties back to defaulting borrowers.

title: Government may roll out flexible pension scheme for all workers under EPFO 3.0 reforms: Report
summary: The proposed contributory pension scheme could cover formal, unorganised, gig and higher-wage workers, with flexible withdrawals and a “Target Retirement Sum” approach.

title: Retirement planning: Why the rule of 1% upgrade works better than a 1% increase in SIP amount
summary: The rule of 1% upgrade encourages investors to increase the share of their income invested by one percentage point every year. A small annual increase in the share of income invested can significantly boost long-term retirement savings.

title: EPF Scheme 2026: Why you must keep 25% of your PF balance before retirement
summary: The new EPF Scheme introduces a 25% minimum balance requirement for partial withdrawals. Here's what the rule means, how eligible member balance is calculated, and when you can withdraw your full PF corpus.

title: ₹25,000 monthly SIP at 12%: Check estimated corpus after 10, 20 and 30 years and see how compounding builds ₹8.74 crore
summary: A ₹25,000 monthly SIP earning an assumed 12% annual return can grow to ₹8.74 crore in 30 years. Check how compounding, understanding of core principles, disciplined investing and staying invested help create long-term wealth for retirement, children's education and financial independence.

title: Sold house for ₹1.10 crore, tax computed on ₹1.96 crore? ITAT explains when Section 54 relief is still available
summary: The Pune ITAT has ruled that capital gains computed using the higher stamp duty value under Section 50C do not automatically deny Section 54 exemption. The tax benefit, however, is available only if the taxpayer satisfies all conditions for claiming the exemption.

title: Health insurance: 63% policyholders unable to switch from their plan — Here's why and key things to keep in mind
summary: An analysis by CoverSure showed that besides under-penetration of health insurance, another growing problem for many policyholders is that coverage exists but as many as 63% are unable to switch plans.

title: Nifty weekly outlook: 24,500 holds the key to next leg of gains; focus on stock-specific bets
summary: The Nifty ended the week with modest gains as the index defended the crucial 23,800–24,000 support zone, keeping the broader technical structure positive. title: Buy & hold? Think again: James ‘RevShark’ DePorre says invest like a shark, not a spectator
summary: Veteran strategist James 'RevShark' DePorre challenges the passive "buy and hold" mantra, arguing investors should stay alert and react to market conditions rather than predict them. Capital preservation and disciplined risk management form the backbone of his five-point investing philosophy.

summary: Axis Bank on Saturday reported a 23% year-on-year increase in standalone quarterly net profit, aided by lower provisions, even as its pre-provision operating profit grew just 1.3%.
